metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the CHANGELOG date instruction: replace `git log -1 --format=%as` (which reads the *previous* commit's date at staging time) with `date -u +%Y-%m-%d` or equivalent to capture today's actual landing date.
evidence: Plan says: 'The date is set from the actual commit date at commit time (`git log -1 --format=%as`), not hardcoded.'

`git log -1 --format=%as` returns the author date of the most recent commit in the working tree — which, at the moment the CHANGELOG is being edited and staged, is still the prior commit (not the release commit that hasn't been made yet).
The release commit is created *after* the CHANGELOG is staged; therefore `git log -1 --format=%as` at edit time yields the wrong (previous) commit's date.
findings.finding: The plan instructs setting the CHANGELOG `[0.8.0]` date via `git log -1 --format=%as`, but this command returns the date of the *previous* (already-landed) commit — not the date of the commit being created. The CHANGELOG must be staged and committed as part of the new commit, so `git log -1` at staging time references the preceding commit (0.7.1 release or later), not the 0.8.0 landing commit itself. The actual landing date should come from the current wall-clock date (e.g. `date -u +%Y-%m-%d`) or be set to today's date at commit time.
findings.location: What / Scope — CHANGELOG.md bullet: 'The date is set from the actual commit date at commit time (`git log -1 --format=%as`), not hardcoded.'
scenarios: Author runs `git log -1 --format=%as` before committing, gets the date of the most recently landed commit (e.g. 2026-07-06 from the 0.7.1 release), inserts that date into the CHANGELOG, and commits — producing `## [0.8.0] - 2026-07-06` (the prior release date) rather than the actual landing date.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the date-setting instruction with: 'Set the date to today's UTC date when editing: `date -u +%Y-%m-%d`.' (After `git commit`, `git log -1 --format=%as HEAD` would give the correct date, but that is too late to edit CHANGELOG before committing.)

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).

verify command `grep -q 'currently 0.8.0' docs/api-stability.md` greps the unanchored word 'currently' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_currently'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"currently":')

verify command `! grep -q 'git push origin main' docs/releasing.md && grep -q 'refs/for/main' docs/releasing.md` greps the unanchored word 'git' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_git'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"git":')
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit manual fallback command/procedure for the `mcp_registry` step to the Failure recovery section (analogous to the `gh release create` command provided for `github_release`).
evidence: Plan quote: 're-run only the failed job, or complete it manually (gh release create v0.8.0 … / re-run the mcp publish step)' — the github_release path gets a concrete CLI command; the mcp path only says 're-run the mcp publish step', which is not a manual fallback.
ABSENCE: No manual CLI command or API call for MCP registry publish is specified.
findings.finding: The failure-recovery section provides an explicit manual fallback command for the `github_release` step (`gh release create v0.8.0 …`) but gives no equivalent manual fallback for the `mcp_registry` step, leaving operators without a concrete recovery path if that job cannot simply be re-run (e.g., the MCP registry API is flaky or the step itself is broken).
findings.location: Failure recovery section — 'complete it manually (gh release create v0.8.0 … / re-run the mcp publish step)'
scenarios: MCP registry API is intermittently unavailable and GitHub Actions re-run also fails; operator has no documented manual path to complete the registration.
The mcp_registry workflow step has a bug that prevents re-runs; operator must publish manually but no command/API is documented in the runbook.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Failure recovery section, after 're-run the mcp publish step', add: 'or publish manually via `<mcp-cli publish --version 0.8.0>` / the MCP registry API (see docs/releasing.md for credentials and endpoint)'.

findings.finding: docs/api-stability.md contains a hard-coded 'currently 0.7.1' prose marker that tests/unit/test_version_sync.py enforces against pyproject.toml at test time. The plan lists only three version manifests to bump (pyproject.toml, server.json, CHANGELOG.md) and omits docs/api-stability.md. If left at 0.7.1 while pyproject.toml is bumped to 0.8.0, `make test` (required by AC) will fail with a version-sync assertion error.
findings.location: What / Scope — three named edit targets; tests/unit/test_version_sync.py; docs/api-stability.md line 3
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What / Scope' section, add a fourth bullet: `docs/api-stability.md` — update the `currently 0.7.1` prose marker to `currently 0.8.0`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] When correcting docs/releasing.md step 1 to use the Gerrit landing flow, also add `docs/api-stability.md` to the `git add` command (and note the `currently X.Y.Z` marker that must be bumped), so the corrected runbook covers all files that test_version_sync.py enforces.
evidence: docs/releasing.md step 1 code block: 'git add pyproject.toml server.json && git commit -m "Release X.Y.Z" && git push origin main'

Plan What/Scope: 'docs/api-stability.md — the prose marker `currently 0.7.1` -> `currently 0.8.0`. This is enforced: tests/unit/test_version_sync.py asserts the doc marker equals pyproject.toml's version'

Plan What/Scope on docs/releasing.md: 'correct the release runbook to the Gerrit flow: step 1 must not say git push origin main … Fix any other step that implies a direct push to main' — omits updating the git add file list
docs/api-stability.md is not mentioned anywhere in docs/releasing.md step 1 or step 1a as a file to bump during a release
findings.finding: The plan's docs/releasing.md correction is scoped only to replacing 'git push origin main' with the Gerrit flow, but the step 1 code block's 'git add' command lists only pyproject.toml and server.json — omitting docs/api-stability.md, which is an enforced version-bump target (test_version_sync.py will fail the Gerrit Verified vote if it is missed). A future release-cutter following the corrected runbook will omit the api-stability.md bump and be blocked by CI, because the runbook never mentions that file in the bump step.
scenarios: A future release-cutter follows the corrected runbook step 1, runs 'git add pyproject.toml server.json' (the corrected Gerrit command), commits, and pushes to Gerrit — the Verified vote fails immediately because test_version_sync.py detects docs/api-stability.md still says 0.8.0 while pyproject.toml says 0.9.0 (or whatever the next version is).
The runbook correction is supposed to make future releases self-contained; leaving api-stability.md out of the bump checklist defeats that goal.
findings.suggested_fix: In docs/releasing.md step 1, change the `git add` line to: `git add pyproject.toml server.json docs/api-stability.md CHANGELOG.md` and add a comment like `# docs/api-stability.md: update "currently X.Y.Z" marker (enforced by test_version_sync.py)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the plan's 'no git-cliff regeneration' instruction with docs/releasing.md step 1a: either add step 1a to the docs/releasing.md correction scope (noting this release hand-promotes [Unreleased] without running `make changelog`), or add an explicit executor note in the plan that step 1a is skipped for this release.
evidence: Plan text: 'The section is already hand-curated so no git-cliff regeneration is needed.'
docs/releasing.md line 257: `make changelog VERSION=vX.Y.Z   # prepends the [X.Y.Z] section from unreleased commits`
Plan scope for docs/releasing.md: 'step 1 must not say git push origin main... consistent with... CONTRIBUTING.md' — no mention of correcting step 1a's git-cliff instruction
An executor following both plan and runbook would see the runbook prescribe git-cliff regeneration while the plan forbids it for this release, with no reconciliation instruction.
findings.finding: The plan says CHANGELOG.md 'is already hand-curated so no git-cliff regeneration is needed' for this release, but the existing docs/releasing.md step 1a (which the plan's scope does NOT instruct to remove or correct) still tells an executor to run `make changelog VERSION=vX.Y.Z` (the git-cliff regeneration path). An executor following the plan AND the in-place runbook would encounter contradictory instructions — the plan prohibits git-cliff regeneration but the unsuppressed runbook step actively prescribes it. This is a pink-elephant/instruction-locality gap: the plan's docs/releasing.md scope only covers fixing `git push origin main` → Gerrit flow, leaving the contradicting step 1a intact.
findings.location: What/Scope: CHANGELOG.md paragraph ('no git-cliff regeneration is needed') vs. docs/releasing.md step 1a (line 247–265: `make changelog VERSION=vX.Y.Z`)
scenarios: Executor runs `make changelog VERSION=v0.8.0` as step 1a instructs, regenerating/overwriting hand-curated CHANGELOG content rather than simply retitling [Unreleased] to [0.8.0].
Executor is confused about which CHANGELOG update process applies and either double-applies or skips the hand-curation step.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What/Scope docs/releasing.md bullet: 'Also correct step 1a to clarify that for releases where [Unreleased] is already hand-curated, the `make changelog` git-cliff step is skipped — the executor simply retitles the section and updates footer links as described above.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the What/Scope `server.json` bullet to say 'every `packages[].version`' (or enumerate all entries) so it matches the AC verify script's `all(...)` check.
evidence: AC verify: '`all(p['version']=='0.8.0' for p in d['packages'])`'
findings.finding: The What/Scope section describes bumping `packages[0].version` (singular, index-zero), but the AC verify script checks `all(p['version']=='0.8.0' for p in d['packages'])` (every entry). An implementer reading only the body text might update only `packages[0]` and miss additional package entries if they exist, while still passing the body description.
findings.location: What/Scope — `server.json` bullet; AC — `server.json` criterion verify script
scenarios: If server.json contains more than one entry under `packages`, an implementer following only the body text would bump only index 0, silently leaving others at 0.7.1; the AC would then fail.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the body bullet from '`packages[0].version` -> `0.8.0`' to 'every entry in `packages[].version` -> `0.8.0`' to match the AC's `all(p['version']=='0.8.0' for p in d['packages'])` guard.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
